name: Canales de Dulla - Valle de Sotocueva — circular desde Puentedey
region_intro: Hiking around Merindad De Valdeporres offers diverse landscapes, from river valleys carved by the Nela and Engaña to the elevated terrain of the Montes del Somo. The region features extensive forests, including ancient oak and beech woodlands, providing varied settings for trails. Impressive karst formations, such as the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey and the Cascada de la Mea, add unique geological interest to the hiking experience. These natural features create a rich environment for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Merindad De Valdeporres The most popular hiking route is Canales de Dulla - Valle de Sotocueva — circular desde Puentedey, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Dulla canyons and the Sotocueva valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Puentedey, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail explores the iconic natural arch and surrounding area of Puentedey. Local hikers also love the CascadaLaMea loop from Puentedey, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through a narrow canyon and oak forest to the Cascada de la Mea, often completed in about 49 minutes. Hiking around Merindad De Valdeporres is defined by river valleys, karst formations, and dense forests, with a network of trails offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdeporres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Merindad De Valdeporres offers a diverse network of nearly 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy walks to more challenging treks through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The hiking trails in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from winding river valleys to impressive karst formations, and the opportunity to explore natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt; and the serene forests.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Yes, Merindad De Valdeporres features several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaLaMea loop from Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 1.8-mile (2.9 km) path that leads through a narrow canyon and oak forest to the beautiful Cascada de la Mea waterfall.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Merindad De Valdeporres are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047088209/canales-de-dulla-valle-de-sotocueva-circular-desde-puentedey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canales de Dulla - Valle de Sotocueva — circular desde Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, covering 8.9 miles (14.3 km) and offering views of the Dulla canyons.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaLaMea loop from Puentedey&lt;/a&gt; takes you directly to the Cascada de la Mea. Additionally, the Cascadas de las Pisas near Soncillo are a natural gem, and you can reach them via a detour on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail between mountains – Las Callejuelas loop from Poblado Portolés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Merindad De Valdeporres is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Nela River, or discover impressive karst formations. The area also features ancient oak and beech forests, river valleys, and elevated viewpoints offering panoramic vistas, such as those from the limestone platform at the foot of "Peña Dulla" on the Sendero PRC-BU-54.

Q: Are there any challenging day hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Merindad De Valdeporres offers difficult routes. One such demanding hike is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muela de Dulla – Canales de Dulla loop from Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 13 miles (21 km) and involves significant elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience with spectacular views.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Most hiking trails in Merindad De Valdeporres are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to respect local regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The best time to go hiking in Merindad De Valdeporres is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, vibrant foliage, and comfortable conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes, from lush river valleys to ancient forests. Summer can also be suitable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. The trail following the old Santander-Mediterráneo railway line, known as La Engaña, leads to tunnels and a settlement built for railway workers, offering a glimpse into the area's industrial past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt; is a significant religious site that can be explored in the vicinity of some trails.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: While public transport options within Merindad De Valdeporres are limited, some villages that serve as trailheads, such as Puentedey, may have infrequent bus services. For greater flexibility in accessing the diverse range of hiking trails, it is generally recommended to travel by car.
tips: Channels that have been formed over the years by the erosion of the waters in these lands that have formed huge channels in what is called the Dulla Mountain. The synchronization of tables and channels originate a different but spectacular landscape!
Church that is located in the old middle of the town of Puentedey and where it rises above the rest of the town's hills

This is considered one of the most beautiful towns in Spain and for good reason. It is crossed by the Río Nela and where the houses are built above a natural cave that has created the passage of the river by making a hole in the stone itself. You can not miss it!
